images.alt: A luminous cutaway illustration of a human cell showing its organelles
images.caption: Cover art: a naturalist's cutaway of a single human cell.
images.prompt: A luminous, elegant scientific-illustration cutaway of a single human cell floating on warm cream paper, in the style of a naturalist's field-guide plate. Show the translucent outer membrane peeled open to reveal the violet nucleus at the center, several orange bean-shaped mitochondria with visible inner folds, a coiled green DNA strand, ribbons of endoplasmic reticulum and a stack of golden Golgi membranes, all softly backlit. Watercolor-and-ink texture, warm palette (teal, coral, violet, amber, olive) on cream, generous whitespace, no text or labels baked in. Target size ~1200x900px, 4:3 landscape; compose as cover art with the cell centred and safe margins - it is letterboxed into a 4:3 box, so keep nothing important near the edges.

summary: A first-principles, city-metaphor tour of the human cell in a Feynman-as-biologist voice, written to excite teens and adults: the membrane, nucleus/DNA, mitochondria, the protein factory, cytoskeleton, recyclers, the cell cycle, and cancer/stem cells. Includes a live 2D membrane animation, a walking-kinesin animation, an interactive 3D mitochondrion, and hand-drawn SVG diagrams of each organelle.
title: Human Cell
